TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Tsou, story: TsouConv-informants
TEXT.citation: Sung, L. M., Lily, I., Hsieh, F., & Lin, Z. (2008). Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics, 6(2.).

S.FORM: eh 'ua ci la c'o mako miski ho lako ahtu emoemo'unu tan'e.
S.PHON: eh ʔus ʦi ɗs ʦʔo msko miski ho ɗsko shtu emoemoʔunu tsnʔe.
S.FORM: eh 'ua ci la c'o mako miski ho lako ahtu emoemo'unu tan'e.
S.PHON: eh ʔus ʦi ɗs ʦʔo msko miski ho ɗsko shtu emoemoʔunu tsnʔe.
S.TRANSL: 你來這裡,至少也在這裡過一夜.
When you come here, I hope that you can at least stay overnight at times.

S.FORM: 'so aavaho man'i ci hioa a'a s'a ahtu meelʉ uh mai no nte to'tohʉngʉ ho 'so ah man'i ci hioa ne tapangʉ.
S.PHON: ʔso ssvsho msnʔi ʦi hios sʔs sʔs shtu meeɗɨ uh msi no nte toʔtohɨŋɨ ho ʔso sh msnʔi ʦi hios ne tspsŋɨ.
S.FORM: 'so aavaho man'i ci hioa a'a s'a ahtu meelʉ uh mai no nte to'tohʉngʉ ho 'so ah man'i ci hioa ne tapangʉ.
S.PHON: ʔso ssvsho msnʔi ʦi hios sʔs sʔs shtu meeɗɨ uh msi no nte toʔtohɨŋɨ ho ʔso sh msnʔi ʦi hios ne tspsŋɨ.
S.TRANSL: 而且有很多工作...我不能照我想的...達邦有很多工作.
Moreover, so much work... I cannot do so as I wish; so much work in TapangU.

S.FORM: 'a te la eno aiti hoci la ake'i ea zikang ho te la ahtu a'ʉmtʉ miski ho na iho bobaito.
S.PHON: ʔs te ɗs eno siti hoʦi ɗs skeʔi es ziksŋ ho te ɗs shtu sʔɨmtɨ miski ho ns iho ɓoɓsito.
S.FORM: 'a te la eno aiti hoci la ake'i ea zikang ho te la ahtu a'ʉmtʉ miski ho na iho bobaito.
S.PHON: ʔs te ɗs eno siti hoʦi ɗs skeʔi es ziksŋ ho te ɗs shtu sʔɨmtɨ miski ho ns iho ɓoɓsito.
S.TRANSL: 這個嘛... 如果我有一點時間我就可以過夜在台北到處看看.

TRANSL.notes: Let's see...if (I) have a little time, (I) can stay overnight and look around (Taipei).

S.TRANSL: Let's see...if have a little time, can stay overnight and look around .

S.FORM: 'a eno 'so a'ʉmtʉ mako ahtu uh ne hnou ho bo no f'ue o'te ahtu buhmohmo ta isi eainca eatataipahu ho la noa es'esmi na ita tan'e.
S.PHON: ʔs eno ʔso sʔɨmtɨ msko shtu uh ne hnou ho ɓo no fʔue oʔte shtu ɓuhmohmo ts isi esinʦs eststsipshu ho ɗs nos esʔesmi ns its tsnʔe.
S.FORM: 'a eno 'so a'ʉmtʉ mako ahtu uh ne hnou ho bo no f'ue o'te ahtu buhmohmo ta isi eainca eatataipahu ho la noa es'esmi na ita tan'e.
S.PHON: ʔs eno ʔso sʔɨmtɨ msko shtu uh ne hnou ho ɓo no fʔue oʔte shtu ɓuhmohmo ts isi esinʦs eststsipshu ho ɗs nos esʔesmi ns its tsnʔe.
S.TRANSL: 當我來到這裡,台北的人讓我很驚訝.
When I arrive here, I feel surprised at people in Taipei.

S.FORM: 'a eno a'ʉmtʉ botngonU 'e eatatiskova tan'evo.
S.PHON: ʔs eno sʔɨmtɨ ɓotŋonu ʔe eststiskovs tsnʔevo.
S.FORM: 'a eno a'ʉmtʉ botngonU 'e eatatiskova tan'evo.
S.PHON: ʔs eno sʔɨmtɨ ɓotŋonu ʔe eststiskovs tsnʔevo.
S.TRANSL: 對,這裡真的有很多人.
Right. There are many people here.

S.FORM: en ne taseona ne mi'o eon to hikooki a.
S.PHON: en ne tsseons ne miʔo eon to hikooki s.
S.FORM: en ne taseona ne mi'o eon to hikooki a.
S.PHON: en ne tsseons ne miʔo eon to hikooki s.
S.TRANSL: 早上我坐飛機.
On that morning I took an airplane.

S.FORM: a'ʉmtʉ.
S.PHON: sʔɨmtɨ.
S.FORM: a'ʉmtʉ.
S.PHON: sʔɨmtɨ.
S.TRANSL: 真的?
Really?

S.FORM: e'vono na'no sʉfeʉngnʉ aʉmta eainca taunona'vʉ 'o hia botngonʉ.
S.PHON: eʔvono nsʔno sɨfeɨŋnɨ sɨmts esinʦs tsunonsʔvɨ ʔo his ɓotŋonɨ.
S.FORM: e'vono na'no sʉfeʉngnʉ aʉmta eainca taunona'vʉ 'o hia botngonʉ.
S.PHON: eʔvono nsʔno sɨfeɨŋnɨ sɨmts esinʦs tsunonsʔvɨ ʔo his ɓotŋonɨ.
S.TRANSL: 雖然很早,我很驚訝怎麼那麼多人.
Although I left very early, I felt amazed that there are a lot of people there.

S.FORM: mo nia'te sʉngcʉngcʉ 'o mo na i'o mo a'ʉmtʉ na'no meoi no hikoki ne taseona.
S.PHON: mo nisʔte sɨŋʦɨŋʦɨ ʔo mo ns iʔo mo sʔɨmtɨ nsʔno meoi no hikoki ne tsseons.
S.FORM: mo nia'te sʉngcʉngcʉ 'o mo na i'o mo a'ʉmtʉ na'no meoi no hikoki ne taseona.
S.PHON: mo nisʔte sɨŋʦɨŋʦɨ ʔo mo ns iʔo mo sʔɨmtɨ nsʔno meoi no hikoki ne tsseons.
S.TRANSL: 早上那個很大的飛機幾乎坐滿.
The very large airplane on that morning was almost full.

S.FORM: uhm 'a isi no eainca to sensei mainci mais'a mo na'no botngonʉ 'e mo na i'e mo smopaeo ta hikoki maitan'e.
S.PHON: uhm ʔs isi no esinʦs to sensei msinʦi msisʔs mo nsʔno ɓotŋonɨ ʔe mo ns iʔe mo smopseo ts hikoki msitsnʔe.
S.FORM: uhm 'a isi no eainca to sensei mainci mais'a mo na'no botngonʉ 'e mo na i'e mo smopaeo ta hikoki maitan'e.
S.PHON: uhm ʔs isi no esinʦs to sensei msinʦi msisʔs mo nsʔno ɓotŋonɨ ʔe mo ns iʔe mo smopseo ts hikoki msitsnʔe.
S.TRANSL: 台大的教授說今天的飛機似乎人很多.
The teacher said it seems that there're quite a lot of people on the airplane today.

S.FORM: 'a os'o eno eainca mo eno a'ʉmtʉ 'a mo.
S.PHON: ʔs osʔo eno esinʦs mo eno sʔɨmtɨ ʔs mo.
S.FORM: 'a os'o eno eainca mo eno a'ʉmtʉ 'a mo.
S.PHON: ʔs osʔo eno esinʦs mo eno sʔɨmtɨ ʔs mo.
S.TRANSL: 我也說,這個真的是...
I also said, this is really...

S.FORM: ne mo'u la aueu uh tan'e a'a moso s'a maica hia botngonʉ.
S.PHON: ne moʔu ɗs sueu uh tsnʔe sʔs moso sʔs msiʦs his ɓotŋonɨ.
S.FORM: ne mo'u la aueu uh tan'e a'a moso s'a maica hia botngonʉ.
S.PHON: ne moʔu ɗs sueu uh tsnʔe sʔs moso sʔs msiʦs his ɓotŋonɨ.
S.TRANSL: 我上次來這裡沒有這麼多人.
When I came here last time, there were not so many like this.

S.FORM: abohtʉ eaeusuhngi.
S.PHON: sɓohtɨ eseusuhŋi.
S.FORM: abohtʉ eaeusuhngi.
S.PHON: sɓohtɨ eseusuhŋi.
S.TRANSL: 很幸運,有位置.

TRANSL.notes: Fortunately, (you) got a seat.

S.TRANSL: Fortunately, got a seat.

S.FORM: ah isi no aeoca ah ah zou 'asvʉta na i'o te'o eusuhngi ta ta eatan'e eatan'e.
S.PHON: sh isi no seoʦs sh sh zou ʔssvɨts ns iʔo teʔo eusuhŋi ts ts estsnʔe estsnʔe.
S.FORM: ah isi no aeoca ah ah zou 'asvʉta na i'o te'o eusuhngi ta ta eatan'e eatan'e.
S.PHON: sh isi no seoʦs sh sh zou ʔssvɨts ns iʔo teʔo eusuhŋi ts ts estsnʔe estsnʔe.
S.TRANSL: 啊...那是事先... 這裡的人是先幫我預定了位置.
My seat has been reserved in advance by the people here.

S.FORM: mhm ko'ko eno 'aa la smoeo no uk'a eusuhngi ho mo na iho mo noavovei.
S.PHON: mhm koʔko eno ʔss ɗs smoeo no ukʔs eusuhŋi ho mo ns iho mo nosvovei.
S.FORM: mhm ko'ko eno 'aa la smoeo no uk'a eusuhngi ho mo na iho mo noavovei.
S.PHON: mhm koʔko eno ʔss ɗs smoeo no ukʔs eusuhŋi ho mo ns iho mo nosvovei.
S.TRANSL: 因此我來回坐不怕沒有位置.
Therefore I will not be afraid of being without a seat when I go back and forth.

S.FORM: 'a ta'u no cu eaica asvʉta na i'o ta'u eusuhngi no ho ta'u cu maine'e ho eofna.
S.PHON: ʔs tsʔu no ʦu esiʦs ssvɨts ns iʔo tsʔu eusuhŋi no ho tsʔu ʦu msineʔe ho eofns.
S.FORM: 'a ta'u no cu eaica asvʉta na i'o ta'u eusuhngi no ho ta'u cu maine'e ho eofna.
S.PHON: ʔs tsʔu no ʦu esiʦs ssvɨts ns iʔo tsʔu eusuhŋi no ho tsʔu ʦu msineʔe ho eofns.
S.TRANSL: 我晚上回家的位置已經預定了.
My seat for going home in the evening has already been reserved.

S.FORM: ta n'a aʉmta ngoh ngohngoseo ho mo maici eahioa co cono euovei cino mhm cono hie.
S.PHON: ts nʔs sɨmts ŋoh ŋohŋoseo ho mo msiʦi eshios ʦo ʦono euovei ʦino mhm ʦono hie.
S.FORM: ta n'a aʉmta ngoh ngohngoseo ho mo maici eahioa co cono euovei cino mhm cono hie.
S.PHON: ts nʔs sɨmts ŋoh ŋohŋoseo ho mo msiʦi eshios ʦo ʦono euovei ʦino mhm ʦono hie.
S.TRANSL: 一天之內來回真的非常累.
The back and forth in one day is really exhausting.

S.FORM: pa'hicocvo hoci eh maka maka peelʉi na ina nai na ihe aʉmta 'ucia tuococosi ho mo mo na ita mo bua cou.
S.PHON: psʔhiʦoʦvo hoʦi eh msks msks peeɗɨi ns ins nsi ns ihe sɨmts ʔuʦis tuoʦoʦosi ho mo mo ns its mo ɓus ʦou.
S.FORM: pa'hicocvo hoci eh maka maka peelʉi na ina nai na ihe aʉmta 'ucia tuococosi ho mo mo na ita mo bua cou.
S.PHON: psʔhiʦoʦvo hoʦi eh msks msks peeɗɨi ns ins nsi ns ihe sɨmts ʔuʦis tuoʦoʦosi ho mo mo ns its mo ɓus ʦou.
S.TRANSL: 真是開玩笑如果我不能真的回答他們想問的關於說鄒語的問題.

TRANSL.notes: If I cannot (answer the question) they really want to ask about Tsou, it will be a joke.

S.TRANSL: If I cannot they really want to ask about Tsou, it will be a joke.

S.FORM: eh uvo honci s'a o'te peelʉi na na su na nte o'te meelʉ ma sia na nte n'a ahtu meelʉ.
S.PHON: eh uvo honʦi sʔs oʔte peeɗɨi ns ns su ns nte oʔte meeɗɨ ms sis ns nte nʔs shtu meeɗɨ.
S.FORM: eh uvo honci s'a o'te peelʉi na na su na nte o'te meelʉ ma sia na nte n'a ahtu meelʉ.
S.PHON: eh uvo honʦi sʔs oʔte peeɗɨi ns ns su ns nte oʔte meeɗɨ ms sis ns nte nʔs shtu meeɗɨ.
S.TRANSL: 你怎麼有可能不會?如果連你都不行,還有誰能?

TRANSL.notes: How could it be possible that you can't? If you cannot (do it well), who else can do it?

S.TRANSL: How could it be possible that you can't? If you cannot , who else can do it?

S.FORM: nanʉhtʉ na i'o os'o hia how teachPF.
S.PHON: nsnɨhtɨ ns iʔo osʔo his ho* tesʦhpf.

TRANSL.notes: 我教的是短的(句子).

S.TRANSL: 我教的是短的 .

TRANSL.notes: What I teach is short (sentences).

S.TRANSL: What I teach is short .

W.TRANSL: 照上下文推論應該是一個受焦動詞

M.TRANSL: 照上下文推論應該是一個受焦動詞

S.FORM: maica ho moso 'enepio 'o mo tuocosʉ mais'a mo ep'ʉcʉ na i'o na i'o hia tuocosʉ ho mo alʉ 'enepio 'o mo maikuyungu.
S.PHON: msiʦs ho moso ʔenepio ʔo mo tuoʦosɨ msisʔs mo epʔɨʦɨ ns iʔo ns iʔo his tuoʦosɨ ho mo sɗɨ ʔenepio ʔo mo msikujuŋu.
S.FORM: maica ho moso 'enepio 'o mo tuocosʉ mais'a mo ep'ʉcʉ na i'o na i'o hia tuocosʉ ho mo alʉ 'enepio 'o mo maikuyungu.
S.PHON: msiʦs ho moso ʔenepio ʔo mo tuoʦosɨ msisʔs mo epʔɨʦɨ ns iʔo ns iʔo his tuoʦosɨ ho mo sɗɨ ʔenepio ʔo mo msikujuŋu.
S.TRANSL: 有很多人問我問題.他們問的內容很複雜,問的情況就像這樣很多人圍著我.
There are many people asking questions. The situation is like this: there are many people surrounding me; what they asked is so complicated.

S.FORM: eh 'a os'o aothoma cohivi na 'e uN maka peelʉi aʉmta eainca yut'inga na ina i'o zou esansana na ihe aʉmta ucia cohivi.
S.PHON: eh ʔs osʔo sothoms ʦohivi ns ʔe un msks peeɗɨi sɨmts esinʦs jutʔiŋs ns ins iʔo zou essnssns ns ihe sɨmts uʦis ʦohivi.
S.FORM: eh 'a os'o aothoma cohivi na 'e uN maka peelʉi aʉmta eainca yut'inga na ina i'o zou esansana na ihe aʉmta ucia cohivi.
S.PHON: eh ʔs osʔo sothoms ʦohivi ns ʔe un msks peeɗɨi sɨmts esinʦs jutʔiŋs ns ins iʔo zou essnssns ns ihe sɨmts uʦis ʦohivi.
S.TRANSL: 我試著去瞭解,至少我能去回答,也就是弄清楚他們真的想要知道的.
I try to understandt, at least I am able to answer, that is to clarify what they really want to know.

S.FORM: os'o eno o'te pnanfi na ina os'o ake'a cohivi ho zou ho eʉsvʉta na ito ihe 'ucia tuocosi.
S.PHON: osʔo eno oʔte pnsnfi ns ins osʔo skeʔs ʦohivi ho zou ho eɨsvɨts ns ito ihe ʔuʦis tuoʦosi.
S.FORM: os'o eno o'te pnanfi na ina os'o ake'a cohivi ho zou ho eʉsvʉta na ito ihe 'ucia tuocosi.
S.PHON: osʔo eno oʔte pnsnfi ns ins osʔo skeʔs ʦohivi ho zou ho eɨsvɨts ns ito ihe ʔuʦis tuoʦosi.
S.TRANSL: 我盡我最大的努力,用我稍微知道的一點知識,去回答他們想問我的.
I try my best to answer what they want to ask with my limited knowledge.

S.FORM: tehin'i s'a eno o'te a'ʉmta zou cohivi na na iko eʉsvUta.
S.PHON: tehinʔi sʔs eno oʔte sʔɨmts zou ʦohivi ns ns iko eɨsvuts.

S.PHON: tehinʔi sʔs eno oʔte sʔɨmts zou ʦohivi ns ns iko eɨsvuts.
S.TRANSL: 他們應該真的知道你所告訴他們的.
They should really understand what you've told them.

S.FORM: ho X man na'no uhm zou man'i 'o lako co'cohivi.
S.PHON: ho * msn nsʔno uhm zou msnʔi ʔo ɗsko ʦoʔʦohivi.

You know a lot.

S.TRANSL: 很幸運他們要問的都事先寫下來,因此我來這裡的時候可以先看.
It's lucky that what they want to ask will be written down in advance, therefore, I can see them in advance when I come here.

Therefore I think maybe I can answer what they want to ask and what they want to know. They will.

S.TRANSL: 如果讓跟你一起在母語工作室工作的伙伴也來這裡當發音人會怎麼樣?我在想有什麼差別嗎?
What if those of you work together at the Mu-yu-kong-cua-shi come here and act as informants? I wonder if there'd be any difference.
